opportunity_model.company_stage: Series B — proven product, scaling GTM. Design hire is likely about maturing the practice, not 0-to-1.
opportunity_model.design_maturity: Emerging — JD mentions 'building the design function' and 'establishing process', suggesting design has been IC-only so far.
opportunity_model.hiring_signal: First design leadership hire. Team of 2 ICs currently. This role is expected to hire 2–3 more within 12 months.
opportunity_model.cultural_tone: Builder-first. JD language is direct and specific about outcomes. No mentions of process or methodology — they want someone who ships.
opportunity_model.recommended_positioning: Founding designer experience + systems at scale — exactly the profile for a team that needs to go from IC-only to a functioning design practice.

act1_who_i_am.positioning_line: I've built design practices from scratch twice — once at a seed-stage developer tools startup, once at a Series B fintech. Meridian is the third.
act1_who_i_am.philosophy_note: A design system is a bet on the future. The tokens and components matter less than the decisions behind them — what's included, what's excluded, and why. I write those decisions down.

act2_what_ive_done.intro_line: Two companies, two design practices built from scratch — here's what that actually looked like.

works.title: Merchant Onboarding — VaultPay
works.framing_angle: Lead with the research-to-decision story. Meridian is a fintech — they'll understand why onboarding conversion matters. The specific decision (progressive disclosure) shows design thinking, not just execution.
works.decision_to_surface: Progressive disclosure over linear checklist — match the document ask to the moment the merchant needs the capability it unlocks
works.outcome_to_surface: Onboarding completion rate: 34% → 71%. Average time to first transaction: 11 days → 3 days.

works.title: VaultPay Design System
works.framing_angle: Frame as an infrastructure decision, not a design deliverable. Built before they had a DS engineer. The bet on token alignment is the interesting decision for a technical audience.
works.decision_to_surface: Built the design system before we had a dedicated DS engineer — mapped Figma tokens 1:1 to engineering tokens already in the codebase
works.outcome_to_surface: Adopted by 8 engineers. No new component built outside the system in 14 months.

works.title: First Designer — Stackform
works.framing_angle: This is the founding designer story. Lead with the research-before-redesign decision — it's the evidence that they think before they execute. Relevant to Meridian because this is exactly the kind of hire they're making.
works.decision_to_surface: Proposed a 3-week discovery sprint before any redesign work — to understand why conversion was low before designing a solution to it
works.outcome_to_surface: Discovery changed the entire redesign brief — the problem was onboarding confusion, not UI quality.

act3_what_ill_bring.intro_line: What the first 90 days looks like — specifically for a team at Meridian's stage.

day_15.title: Map the current state
day_15.body: Before proposing anything, I want to understand what exists — the product surface, the design files, how engineering and design currently hand off, and where the two current ICs feel most constrained. I'll run structured 1:1s with both designers and three product managers in the first two weeks. The output is a written map of the design practice as it exists today — not a roadmap, just an honest inventory.
day_15.credibility_anchor: Did this at both Stackform and VaultPay — the inventory always reveals the real problem, which is rarely what the hiring manager described.

day_30.title: Ship one thing and establish the review process
day_30.body: By day 30 I want one shipped design contribution — not a system or a strategy document, an actual product change. Alongside that: a lightweight design review process that both ICs buy into. Weekly, async-first, with a written decision log. The goal is to establish that design decisions at Meridian are documented and reasoned, not just made.
day_30.credibility_anchor: Built the design review process at VaultPay in month 2. It's still running 18 months later.

day_90.title: Design system foundation and first hire brief
day_90.body: By day 90 the team should have a token set that engineering has adopted, even if the component library is minimal. And I should have a clear point of view on the first additional hire — what skill the team is missing, what kind of designer fills it, and what good looks like for that role. That brief goes to the founders before I write a single job description.
day_90.credibility_anchor: Built VaultPay's design system v1 in 6 weeks while shipping product work. Hired two designers at VaultPay — both are still there.

cold_message.hook: I've built design practices from scratch at two fintech companies — once as sole designer, once as the first design lead. The VaultPay onboarding work took completion rate from 34% to 71%.
cold_message.body: I put together a short page for this application — it covers the specific work and what the first 90 days would look like at Meridian's stage. Easier to read than a CV.

tone_notes: Meridian's JD is direct and outcome-focused. Match that energy — no hedging, no filler. Lead with decisions and numbers.
